sample_size.source_quote: A total of 72 patients with sepsis in ICU were included for analysis.

patient_age_mean.source_quote: The mean ages of the survivors and non-survivors were 71.07 ± 7.52 and 71.45 ± 6.89 years, respectively (P = 0.822).

sofa_score_baseline.source_quote: SOFA score, 10.62 ± 1.67 vs. 7.83 ± 2.29, respectively; P < 0.001 for both

icu_mortality_percent.source_quote: Among 72 patients with sepsis, there were 42 (58.3%) non-survivors and 30 (41.7%) survivors.

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Peak Systolic Velocity (PSV) of superior mesenteric artery
mortality_predictors.timing: within first 24 hours of ICU admission
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: APACHE II score, SOFA score, lactate, EDV, RI
mortality_predictors.source_quote: multivariate analysis suggested that low PSV was an independent risk factor for mortality, and the adjusted odds ratio was 0.295 (95% CI: 0.094–0.925). The ROC analysis showed that the PSV (AUC = 0.99; sensitivity and specificity were 0.99 and 0.96, respectively) had good predictive value for mortality in sepsis.

mortality_predictors.timing: within first 24 hours of ICU admission
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Univariate analysis revealed that the APACHE II score, SOFA score, lactate, low PSV, EDV, and RI were potential risk factors for mortality in sepsis

mortality_predictors.timing: within first 24 hours of ICU admission
